subjects.improbCaption: chance this cross-category record is luck
subjects.heroSentence: This account won 69 of 99 bets across markets the blended odds priced at about 49 percent — roughly 48.97 expected by luck (4.55 standard deviations above chance, about 1 in 369.3K). A near-perfect record across diverse, moderate-odds markets is the cross-category signature the long-shot test cannot see. Consistent with informed trading — not proof of it.

scorecard.numbers: Won 78 of 128 bets across markets the blended odds priced at ~46% — about 58.3 expected by luck (4.4σ above chance, ≈ 1 in 193.4K). A near-perfect record across diverse, moderate-odds markets the long-shot test can't see.

subjects.heroSentence: This account won 69 of 119 bets across markets the blended odds priced at about 45 percent — roughly 53.83 expected by luck (3.78 standard deviations above chance, about 1 in 12.5K). A near-perfect record across diverse, moderate-odds markets is the cross-category signature the long-shot test cannot see. Consistent with informed trading — not proof of it.

scorecard.formula: Poisson-binomial: z = (wins − Σpᵢ) / √Σpᵢ(1−pᵢ) over ALL resolved bets at their own odds
scorecard.numbers: Won 69 of 119 bets across markets the blended odds priced at ~45% — about 53.8 expected by luck (3.8σ above chance, ≈ 1 in 12.5K). A near-perfect record across diverse, moderate-odds markets the long-shot test can't see.

scorecard.formula: average of the market's odds at entry
scorecard.numbers: Average entry odds ~13% across 82 bets — concentrated in long shots (≤20%).

scorecard.formula: age = first bet block − funding block
scorecard.numbers: Account first bet ~2h after funding, with no prior transactions — a purpose-built wallet.

scorecard.formula: winning bets bought right before the price-shock
scorecard.numbers: 1 of 4 winning long-shots were bought within 72h of resolution (soonest 2d before) — entering a ~7% outcome right before it resolved is the informed-entry signature.

scorecard.formula: distinct surprising events won over time
scorecard.numbers: Won against the odds in 21 separate events — being early-and-right on multiple unrelated surprises is a repeat-offender pattern, not one lucky hit.

scorecard.formula: largest event position ÷ this wallet's median bet
scorecard.numbers: Largest position $5,174 is ~51× this wallet's median bet ($101) — an informed bet that dwarfs its own trading norm (the within-trader size anomaly).

subjects.confidenceLimiter: the cross-category test uses a normal approximation over mixed odds; it ranks how improbable the record is, it does not prove intent

subjects.heroSentence: This account won 5 of 5 long-shot bets that the market gave roughly a 18 percent chance. By luck you would expect about 0.9 wins. A record this strong is consistent with informed trading — not proof of it.

scorecard.formula: age = first bet block − funding block
scorecard.numbers: Account first bet ~0h after funding, with no prior transactions — a purpose-built wallet.

scorecard.formula: chance of ≥ 5 wins in 5 tries at 18% each
scorecard.numbers: Won 5 of 5 independent bets the market priced at ~18% — about 0.9 expected by luck. Probability by chance ≈ 1 in 5.3K (3 correlated bets de-correlated).

scorecard.formula: max(YES, NO stake) ÷ total staked
scorecard.numbers: 100% of $12,960 staked one direction (YES), 30% in a single event cluster — the un-hedged, single-thesis concentration of someone betting what they already know.

subjects.confidenceLimiter: the binomial test assumes each bet is an independent event; correlated events are de-correlated before scoring

subjects.improbCaption: chance this cross-category record is luck
subjects.heroSentence: This account won 70 of 86 bets across markets the blended odds priced at about 66 percent — roughly 56.52 expected by luck (3.52 standard deviations above chance, about 1 in 4.6K). A near-perfect record across diverse, moderate-odds markets is the cross-category signature the long-shot test cannot see. Consistent with informed trading — not proof of it.

scorecard.formula: Poisson-binomial: z = (wins − Σpᵢ) / √Σpᵢ(1−pᵢ) over ALL resolved bets at their own odds
scorecard.numbers: Won 70 of 86 bets across markets the blended odds priced at ~66% — about 56.5 expected by luck (3.5σ above chance, ≈ 1 in 4.6K). A near-perfect record across diverse, moderate-odds markets the long-shot test can't see.

scorecard.formula: realized win rate − payoff-implied break-even rate
scorecard.numbers: Won 67% — break-even at these entry prices is ~22% (you must win at your average buy price just to not lose money). +44 points above break-even.

scorecard.formula: winning bets bought right before the price-shock
scorecard.numbers: 1 of 3 winning long-shots were bought within 72h of resolution (soonest 11h before) — entering a ~16% outcome right before it resolved is the informed-entry signature.

scorecard.formula: distinct surprising events won over time
scorecard.numbers: Won against the odds in 15 separate events — being early-and-right on multiple unrelated surprises is a repeat-offender pattern, not one lucky hit.

subjects.improbCaption: chance this cross-category record is luck
subjects.heroSentence: This account won 97 of 101 bets across markets the blended odds priced at about 89 percent — roughly 89.58 expected by luck (3.24 standard deviations above chance, about 1 in 1.7K). A near-perfect record across diverse, moderate-odds markets is the cross-category signature the long-shot test cannot see. Consistent with informed trading — not proof of it.

scorecard.formula: Poisson-binomial: z = (wins − Σpᵢ) / √Σpᵢ(1−pᵢ) over ALL resolved bets at their own odds
scorecard.numbers: Won 97 of 101 bets across markets the blended odds priced at ~89% — about 89.6 expected by luck (3.2σ above chance, ≈ 1 in 1.7K). A near-perfect record across diverse, moderate-odds markets the long-shot test can't see.

scorecard.formula: average of the market's odds at entry
scorecard.numbers: Average entry odds ~10% across 12 bets — concentrated in long shots (≤20%).

scorecard.formula: age = first bet block − funding block
scorecard.numbers: Account first bet ~0h after funding, with no prior transactions — a purpose-built wallet.

scorecard.formula: realized win rate − payoff-implied break-even rate
scorecard.numbers: Won 38% — break-even at these entry prices is ~10% (you must win at your average buy price just to not lose money). +27 points above break-even.

scorecard.formula: z = (profit − market mean) ÷ market SD vs peers
scorecard.numbers: Profited 14.2 standard deviations more than the other traders in the same market — a cross-sectional profit outlier; here it only corroborates a wallet already flagged by the statistical record.

scorecard.formula: distinct surprising events won over time
scorecard.numbers: Won against the odds in 4 separate events — being early-and-right on multiple unrelated surprises is a repeat-offender pattern, not one lucky hit.

scorecard.formula: largest event position ÷ this wallet's median bet
scorecard.numbers: Largest position $119,267 is ~65× this wallet's median bet ($1,830) — an informed bet that dwarfs its own trading norm (the within-trader size anomaly).

scorecard.formula: largest event position ÷ this wallet's median bet

scorecard.numbers: Profited 5.4 standard deviations more than the other traders in the same market — a cross-sectional profit outlier; here it only corroborates a wallet already flagged by the statistical record.

subjects.heroSentence: This account won 8 of 16 long-shot bets that the market gave roughly a 19 percent chance. By luck you would expect about 3 wins. A record this strong is consistent with informed trading — not proof of it.

scorecard.formula: average of the market's odds at entry
scorecard.numbers: Average entry odds ~20% across 19 bets — concentrated in long shots (≤20%).

scorecard.formula: bets held to the end / total bets
scorecard.numbers: 19 of 19 positions held to resolution (100%) — held ≥90% of positions to the outcome; the conviction signature.

scorecard.formula: chance of ≥ 8 wins in 16 tries at 19% each
scorecard.numbers: Won 8 of 16 independent bets the market priced at ~19% — about 3 expected by luck. Probability by chance ≈ 1 in 216 (3 correlated bets de-correlated).

scorecard.formula: one large long-shot win held to resolution
scorecard.numbers: a single $20,075 bet at ~14% that paid ~$143,393 — a high-conviction long-shot concentrated on one outcome, the single-event insider signature (the binomial needs ≥5 independent events; this is the confluence path).

scorecard.formula: largest event position ÷ this wallet's median bet
scorecard.numbers: Largest position $598,780 is ~113× this wallet's median bet ($5,280) — an informed bet that dwarfs its own trading norm (the within-trader size anomaly).

scorecard.formula: winning long-shots bought within hours of resolution
scorecard.numbers: 1 of 2 winning long-shots were bought within 72h of resolution (soonest 40h before) — entering a ~18% outcome right before it resolved is the informed-entry signature.

scorecard.formula: z = (this wallet's profit − market mean) ÷ market SD, over peers in the SAME market
scorecard.numbers: Profited 25.7 standard deviations more than the other traders in the same market — a cross-sectional profit outlier; here it only corroborates a wallet already flagged by the statistical record.

permutation.statement: Across 5712 flagged bets the market priced at an average of 51.9%, the flagged wallets won 3717 where the market's own odds expect about 2965 — 26.9 standard deviations above chance (p < 1e-4, 10,000-shuffle permutation test). Consistent with informed trading across the set — not proof for any one wallet.
